title: Index
summary: Introduces the ambient 0, base, and infinity index types and formal normal coordinates used by component calculations.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Introduces the ambient 0, base, and infinity index types and formal normal coordinates used by component calculations.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/FG/Index.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Ambient normal-form indices: 0, base indices, and infinity. Its constructors are: zero with arguments : Ambient Index ι; base with arguments : ι maps to Ambient Index ι; inf with arguments : Ambient Index ι.
correspondence.leanType: inductive AmbientIndex (ι : Type u) : Type u

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The Kronecker delta on ambient normal-form indices.
correspondence.leanType: def kronecker [DecidableEq ι] (I J : AmbientIndex ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For I of type Ambient Index ι, kronecker I I equals 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_self [DecidableEq ι] (I : AmbientIndex ι) : kronecker I I = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: kronecker ι : equals ι zero inf equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_zero_inf [DecidableEq ι] : kronecker (ι := ι) zero inf = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: kronecker ι : equals ι inf zero equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_inf_zero [DecidableEq ι] : kronecker (ι := ι) inf zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For i of type ι, kronecker zero base i equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_zero_base [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : kronecker zero (base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For i of type ι, kronecker base i zero equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_base_zero [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : kronecker (base i) zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A chosen-scale normal-form coordinate triple (t, x, rho). Its mathematical data are: t: the real numbers; x: M; rho: the real numbers.

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
